metadata.title: Singine Agent Contract for the Collibra Repository
metadata.purpose: Define the common cross-agent working contract for this repository, with a small agent-specific extension layer for Codex, Claude, and future agentic systems including Singine itself.

common.summary: This repository is the system of record for Collibra-specific code, metamodel abstractions, operating-model import/export workflows, Edge integrations, and the implementation behind the `singine collibra ...` command family.

boundary.rule: Implement Collibra REST, SDK, CLI, MCP, operating-model, Edge, and tenant-specific governance logic here.
Expose Collibra-aware logic to Singine through `singine-collibra/python/singine_collibra/` rather than duplicating it in Singine.
Keep Singine focused on execution, privileges, orchestration, and general runtime publication concerns.
Prefer SilkPage for transformation-heavy XML/XSLT/XPath/RDF/TTL/SPARQL/SQL/GraphQL pipelines when that work is not intrinsically Collibra-specific.
Treat the Collibra metamodel and its governed identifiers as a canonical alignment contract across repositories and agents.

change-policy.rule: Default to adding or updating tests for every functional change.
Prefer Singine-level tests and MCP-interface tests when the feature is exposed through `singine collibra ...`.
Update documentation in parallel with code changes: XML model/command docs, OpenAPI descriptions when applicable, man pages, and Markdown pages.
If tests or documentation are intentionally omitted, explain that explicitly in the final response.
When a change introduces or modifies a CLI surface, keep command registration, docs/xml, man pages, and Markdown usage examples aligned.

metamodel-policy.rule: New features should explain how they map to the Collibra metamodel, operating model, assignments, views, or governed identifiers.
Prefer loading the exported operating-model package from `~/ws/today/metamodel/` when available, and use hand-written fallbacks only as secondary sources.
Metamodel visualisation, import, and export features should preserve interchangeability with Singine-friendly in-memory opmodel projections.

verification.rule: Run the smallest meaningful verification for the change, and prefer executable checks over pure inspection.
For Python changes, at minimum run syntax validation and targeted execution paths when possible.
For command-surface changes, verify the parser wiring or command output path directly.

agent.name: codex
agent.title: CODEX.md - Collibra Repository Contract
agent.intro: This file gives Codex-specific emphasis while inheriting the shared Singine agent contract for the repository.

specific.rule: Bias toward implementing the requested change end-to-end, including tests and docs, rather than stopping at design notes.
When patching code, keep generated command/docs artifacts aligned in the same change whenever feasible.
Use the shared XML contract as the source of truth for this file and keep agent-specific additions small.

agent.name: claude
agent.title: CLAUDE.md - Collibra Repository Contract
agent.intro: This file gives Claude-specific emphasis while inheriting the shared Singine agent contract for the repository.

specific.rule: Retain higher-level repository walkthrough context, but keep the shared operational contract authoritative.
When describing a change, include the metamodel and command-surface implications, not just implementation detail.
Use the shared XML contract as the source of truth for this file and keep agent-specific additions small.

agent.name: generic
agent.title: AGENTS.md - Cross-Agent Contract
agent.intro: This file is the generic entry point for agents and LLM-based tools that do not have their own dedicated repository instruction file yet.

specific.rule: Assume the shared 80% contract is mandatory and keep system-specific behavior in a thin compatibility layer.
New agent integrations should extend the XML contract rather than forking repository policy into unrelated markdown files.
